programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: When Lynne and Paul met while sailing five years ago, neither had plans to fall in love, let alone relocate to the wilds of Tasmania to build a home. But that all changes for the Melbourne couple when they visit the Apple Isle and find a beautiful stretch of coastal wilderness with panoramic views over the Tasman Sea. With grand plans to build a huge Modern home, they decide that Paul will project manage construction. But living onsite for months on end in a battered up old caravan will test his skills, endurance and ultimately his health.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Furniture maker Lucinda, son Dan and daughter Rosie build a wooden armadillo house. They need to find innovative ways to transport materials without damaging tree roots without heavy machinery.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Mary and Tom were looking to sell after Mary was diagnosed with a degenerative disease that affects her mobility. But after a year on the market, the couple need help so they can downsize to a more suitable home.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Property in the coastal town of Austinmer is so hot that buying another house before you sell is considered the norm. Judy and Graham Mahoney never dreamed they would have trouble selling their three-bedroom weatherboard cottage, so they went ahead and purchased a new home before they sold. After 10 months on the market, a failed auction, and 80 inspections, they have a big problem and an even bigger bridging loan. Buyers are turned off by the bad presentation, poor level of maintenance, and the lack of a communal bathroom. Despite the feedback, Judy and Graham refuse to budge from their $700,000 asking price. They need the money to renovate their new home, but as each month passes, the couple slip further into debt. They have loans on both properties and need to find almost $6000 each month just to stay afloat.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan and the team are creating a garden dedicated to the celebration of all things textural. Full of plants that you want to stroke, touch and feel, this garden is being created for a hardworking support worker.

programme.title: Christmas At Stately Home
programme.desc: The story of how Castle Howard gets their estate ready for the Christmas period.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: In Week three, the sewers tackle travel-themed challenges: a summer blouse from tablecloths, nautical bags in the Transformation, and Riviera-inspired outfits in Made to Measure. Who will impress?

programme.title: Celebrity Sewing Bee Festive Specials
programme.desc: The Vivienne, Lesley Joseph, Sabrina Grant and Sally Philips must make a child's party skirt in the pattern challenge and turn old clothes into a recognisable pantomime character in the transformation challenge. Finally, for the made to measure challenge they are asked to create a New Year's Eve party dress that evokes memories of a particular New Year's Eve, with surprising results.

programme.title: Inside The Ambulance
programme.desc: Join the paramedics as they treat a woman trapped in her car after a crash, a man with a hernia and a poorly three-year-old. Plus, they're called to an elderly woman who's badly cut her legs.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Emergency
programme.desc: Mark is rushed to A&E struggling to breathe. Thomas is in with a high temperature that may be linked to his rare blood condition and Katrina is treated after she injured her knee climbing over a fence.

programme.title: Sex, Mind And the Menopause
programme.desc: Davina McCall explores how menopause can affect the mind as well as the body, with memory loss and brain fog decimating women at work. And the latest on hormone therapy and women and testosterone.

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex is hit hard by the struggles of patients going through IVF as she meets Jasmine and Rumen who are trying for their second child, after Rumen's cancer and Jasmine's previous miscarriage.

programme.title: Home Greek Home
programme.desc: Sarah Moore follows her dream of buying a house in Greece, but has never seen it in person. Upon arriving, Sarah discovers the complications of renovations in a country with different building rules and regulations.

programme.title: Grand Designs House of the Year
programme.desc: This episode looks at five longlisted houses showcasing exceptional materials and craftsmanship, before revealing the winner of the 2022 prize.

programme.title: Millionaire Holiday Home Swap
programme.desc: In the lovingly restored Chateau de Vezins in France, we meet a guest whose daily health routine puts the cat among the pigeons. On top of that, the private chef has left, leaving the cooking to the guest.

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.sub-title: Music Week And A Mini Musical Instrument
programme.desc: Siobhan McSweeney hosts Music Week as the potters are challenged to sculpt a life like bust of a music legend, kick starting with a surprise special message from Dolly Parton.

programme.desc: A new courthouse drama that follows the chaotic, hopeful and sometimes absurd lives of its judges, prosecutors and public defenders, as they work with bailiffs, clerks and cops to get justice for the people of Los Angeles amidst a flawed legal process. Among those working within the court system is newly appointed Judge Lola Carmichael, a highly regarded and impressive deputy district attorney who doesn't intend to sit back on the bench in her new role, but instead leans in, immediately pushing the boundaries and challenging the expectations of what a judge can be. Those offering Lola counsel are her roguish best friend and colleague, Mark Callan, a successful deputy district attorney who now must adjust to the shifting boundaries in their personal and professional relationship; Supervisory Judge Lisa Benner, an influential, powerful figure tasked with overseeing Lola's transition into her new job; and Lola's efficient, pragmatic judicial assistant, Sherri Kansky.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.sub-title: A Tiger Chase And A Tight Place
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Carl Andrews gets behind the wheel to transport a sizeable load, Russell's mobile classroom is shaken, and the Ferrises manage to dodge raging bush fires.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons writer's room is the stuff of TV legend. It would churn out hilarious scripts like a Ford automobile assembly line, turning what could have been a niche, off-beat miss into a surprise hit. The phenomenal success of the show has almost always been attributed to the irreverent and groundbreaking writing, but while the writers toiled away, tensions rose among the three creators.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie, along with guest performer Jeff Davis and star guest TV presenter and former boxing champion Laila Ali. In a succession of brilliant games, the team provide weird medical exams and disastrous job interviews in Scenes from a Hat, present a stream of inventive suggestions for some bizarre props, and Wayne and Jeff tackle the subject of acne in the style of mariachi and Les Miserables in Greatest Hits. Laila Ali also joins in the fun, acting out a modelling photo shoot scenario with Wayne providing her voice and learning to box with her grizzled trainer Ryan in Helping Hands.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie, along with guest performer Jeff Davis and special guests Mary Killman and Mariya Koroleva from the US Olympic synchronised swimming team. There's a mixture of brilliant games - both old and new - for the performers to get stuck into: Hollywood Director Colin coaches the others through a criminal break-in scene, a seductive housewife tempts a hunky electrician in the ingenious Sideways Scene, and an eventful snowboarding session tests the performers' memories and wits to the full in the always challenging Forward Rewind. Special guests Mary and Mariya from the US Olympic synchronised swimming team also get to join in the fun, acting as a variety of props in a dense jungle adventure scene and being serenaded by boy banders Jeff and Wayne.

programme.title: Idiot TV
programme.desc: The very best falls, faults and fails have been curated and exhibited, using slow motion, rewinds and close-ups to enhance their full-blown stupidity for the silver screen.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: It's the final episode of the season and one of the five intrepid comics will win big and get to take home Greg Davies' gorgeous golden head.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers. It is vast, untamed, and home to some of the toughest to reach places on Earth. For the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, or braving a continent's worth of brutal bitumen and desolate dirt, this is a land that can destroy trucks and truckers in a heartbeat. But for the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again, for a season of dirt, dramas and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Mark brings a city to a standstill with his multimillion-dollar load, Yogi is on an antiques roadshow where time is of the essence, and Turbo is in a dirt road marathon dogfight.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Danielle scour a mind-blowing Detroit home that features floor-to-ceiling folk art relics and a sensory-overload attic packed with some naughty treasure. Down the road, a random lead at a Michigan roadstop leads to a late collector's mighty stockpile where Jersey and Robbie nerd out on antique tools and one-of-a-kind accessories for a Model T Ford.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: We Don't Use The 'S' Word Around Here
programme.desc: Rolling into Buena Park, Brandi & Jarrod pick up what may be the best of the worst. Kenny and his cousin glide through a locker that's only half decent. Ivy comes to the auction dressed to kill. Mary tries to turn up the volume.

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers. It is vast, untamed, and home to some of the toughest to reach places on Earth. For the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, or braving a continent's worth of brutal bitumen and desolate dirt, this is a land that can destroy trucks and truckers in a heartbeat. But for the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again, for a season of dirt, dramas and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Mark brings a city to a standstill with his multimillion-dollar load, Yogi is on an antiques roadshow where time is of the essence, and Turbo is in a dirt road marathon dogfight.

programme.desc: As the team attempt to save The Bride and Nina, GI Robot relives his glory days, and finally gets his wish.

programme.title: Love It or List It

programme.title: Love It or List It Vancouver
programme.desc: Liza and Shawn can't agree on how to fix their cold, closed-off character home. Can Todd persuade Liza to move with a hassle-free home? Or will Jillian open things up and warm Shawn to the idea of staying?

programme.title: Below Deck
programme.desc: The stews are extremely excited to welcome aboard a family of gorgeous charter guests, including a Ryan Gosling doppelgnger, to celebrate their dad's 50th birthday. Leon commits a big faux pas by ignoring a guestas request to refrain from serving rabbit for dinner, bringing up a traumatic childhood memory for the primary's wife. Connies deckhand skills impress Eddie and Captain Lee, but Emile is feeling underappreciated. Eddie begins to question his closeness with Rocky, but there's more at risk than personal relationships when a fire breaks out in the galley putting the whole boat in danger.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: The respite doesn't last long; in September the men learn they're being airlifted to Holland as part of Operation Market-Garden, a joint American-British offensive that aims at securing quick access to Germany and ending the war by Christmas. Unlike Normandy, the daytime parachute drop is executed smoothly, and the company is greeted as heroes as they enter the town of Eindhoven after minimal resistance. All that changes when the company marches to Nuenen, where Easy is met by a brutal attack that ends with Compton wounded, a retreat ordered, and 'Bull' Randleman stranded in the town overnight. It's a sobering and bloody initiation to combat for the replacements.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As Winters labours over a report on the dike mission, Heyliger leads a rescue of British soldiers escaping from the besieged town of Arnhem. An anxious Winters worries about Easy (now one of three companies he helps command), especially after Heyliger is shot and wounded by a nervous sentry. After moving off the line to France, Nixon insists that Winters take a break and see Paris. Winters is haunted there by the memory of a young German he killed at close range on the dike. As he returns to the Company at Mourmelon, news comes of a massive German counterattack in the Ardennes Forest. Winters helps Easy race there to hold the line, his men ill-equipped for the cold and the battle ahead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Medic Eugene Roe is overwhelmed, on edge and close to combat exhaustion, when he finds friendship with a Belgian nurse. Easy Company spends a miserable Christmas in the trenches, but is buoyed after receiving the news that the German army's demand for surrender was met with General McAuliffe's defiant answer: "Nuts!"

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Several are killed and wounded in fierce shelling, compounded by the incompetence of their new commander, Lieutenant Dike, about whom Winters can do nothing. Easy takes Foy, but at an enormous cost.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Hank Jones, fresh from West Point and eager for combat experience, volunteers to lead, though he must convince a sceptical Winters. Also assigned to the patrol is Private David Webster, back in Easy after rehabilitation of an injury. While successful, the mission costs a soldier's life, prompting Winters to ignore the order to send out a second patrol the next night.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: A patrol in a nearby forest discovers an abandoned Nazi concentration camp, still filled with emaciated prisoners. The local citizenry, unbelievably disavowing knowledge of its existence, is made to clean it up. Suddenly, news arrives from Berlin: Hitler is dead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. Despite the fact that VE (Victory in Europe) Day has passed, Easy faces the prospect of imminent deployment to the Pacific Theatre. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Following a shooting in a nightclub, which resulted in the murder of a famous model, the police investigate whether this was a random or targeted shooting.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Lot Of Band-Aids And The Cooper Surrender
programme.desc: Sheldon meets Oscar and Darren, undergrads in the dorm room next door to his. Finding they have many things in common like video games, Sheldon likes hanging out with them. Meanwhile, George's job is at risk due to parents who are unhappy with the football team's performance. George's meeting with them doesn't go well. A stressed-out George lets Sheldon stay overnight to play Dungeons & Dragons at the dorm without consulting Mary. Though annoyed, Mary is able to talk to George and give him a sincere hug when she learns what is bothering him. Mary later goes to get Sheldon herself, as he is about to vomit from too much candy and root beer. Also, after clothes shopping, Missy is made fun of at school and accidentally injures herself trying to shave her legs. Connie patches her up and promises to show her how to do it safely, cheering Missy up.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Lainey surprises everyone with her college announcement, prompting Barry to vow to follow in the footsteps of Doogie Howser MD and become a doctor while still in high school.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam tells a lie that lands him a prom date with an older classmate. Barry asks Erica to help make the dance a night to remember for him and Lainey.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake thinks Boyle might have met his soulmate in Genevieve, an art gallery owner, and he tries to help set them up. Meanwhile, Rosa deals with inter-precinct theft and Holt seeks Amy's help with an image campaign.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When investigating a serial killer, Jake enlists Holt's help in an attempt to solve the case off the radar. At the precinct, Rosa and Amy are forced to deal with demands from an old adversary and Terry discovers a new obsession.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: While trying to restart his chiropractic business, Alan stumbles on a money-making goldmine that involves lonely women and a clueless Walden.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden takes Jenny and Barry with him on a road trip to reconnect with his past while Alan and Gretchen's relationship hits a rough spot over Alan's assumed identity.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan finally comes clean with Larry about he and Lyndsey. The fallout has ramifications for both weddings, with a twist no one saw coming.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: When guest star Bette Midler is injured during a softball game, Jerry and George are accused of conspiring to get her understudy the lead role in a musical.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry and George reevaluate their relationships with women and make a pact to change their ways, though Jerry doesn't know it. Elaine has a problem sleeping in her new apartment because of a dog that constantly barks nearby.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie's work has her mentor a troubled teen, but when actually hanging out with the teen, Carrie begins to become more pressured into her old misbehaving self.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Arthur accidentally starts a fire in Doug and Carrie's basement, so Carrie then begins to think that her and Doug should find him a retirement home to go live in. When Doug starts to clean out the basement by removing Arthur's things, Doug sees that Arthur used to be an actor and he finds out that he gave up the career for Carrie. This gives Doug second thoughts on giving Arthur the boot.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Pink Cadillac And A Glorious Tribal Dance
programme.desc: Mary runs into Mr. Lundy who has a pink Cadillac from selling Mary Kay makeup products. He talks Mary into selling for him. She initially gets one sale to the high school librarian Ms. Hutchins. Mr. Lundy talks Mary into selling to the women in her Bible Study group. Mary succeeds, but later has a guilt-ridden dream for doing so, and quits being a seller. Sheldon is on Spring Break from college when he hears about a three-day comic convention. Unable to get an adult to go with him, Sheldon decides to rebel and take a bus to the convention. Seeing someone from his local comic shop and his old science teacher Mr. Givens, they debate the merits of Lost in Space, Star Trek and The Next Generation. Sheldon enjoys the trip though gets in trouble afterwards for sneaking out.

programme.title: Will & Grace

programme.desc: When Amy breaks up with Robert, the entire family blames Raymond because he told his brother that he feels like "a hostage" and that Debra only keeps him alive "to open jars and kill bugs".

programme.sub-title: The Third Wheel Gets The Grace

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present, pointing to the quirky, the surprising and the beautiful lying just offshore.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo visits a station fit for royalty in Windsor, views the Maidenhead Railway Bridge an engineering triumph built by Isambard Kingdom Brunel to span the River Thames at Maidenhead, and tries his hand at collecting the mail 'Victorian style' on a steam-powered travelling post office.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo tastes a Victorian superfood in Alton, explores the fascinating Whitchurch silk mill, untouched for over 150 years, and tries his hand at driving a steam train on the challenging Watercress Line.

programme.title: Comedy Legends

programme.desc: In the era of hit movies such as Titanic, Braveheart and Saving Private Ryan, a host of new composers began to emerge.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: With the help of archaeologists and scientists, this series looks to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the world's most fascinating treasures, shining a light on Ancient History and playing into people's thirst for conspiracy theories.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: To celebrate the bicentenary of the birth of John Ruskin, our winning artist will visit some of this Victorian art critic's most-loved Venetian architectural icons, to photograph, sketch and take artistic inspiration, before returning to the UK to create a landscape artwork that will be unveiled by Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell, and hang at RIBA's prestigious London HQ.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Presenters Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan return for the tenth series of Portrait Artist Of The Year. In a special opening episode to celebrate the 10th anniversary, eight previous winners return to paint an exceptional sitter, Dame Judi Dench in a battle to become Portrait Artist Of The Decade.

programme.title: The World According To Amazon
programme.desc: An exploration into Amazon's business models and practices, from its inception to present day.

programme.title: Food, Inc. America's Food Industry
programme.desc: An unflattering look inside America's corporate controlled food industry. The current method of raw food production is largely a response to the growth of the fast food industry since the 1950s. The production of food overall has more drastically changed since that time than the several thousand years prior.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: In the 1980s, Penthouse is at its height as one of the most successful magazines in America. Following the release of Bob Guccione's infamous Roman porno epic "Caligula," Penthouse faces a moral backlash from both the religious right and feminist left. Always keen to push boundaries, Guccione publishes nude pictures of the first black Miss America, Vanessa Williams, triggering a public outcry while sending sales skyrocketing.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: In the 1980s, Penthouse is at its height as one of the most successful magazines in America. Following the release of Bob Guccione's infamous Roman porno epic "Caligula," Penthouse faces a moral backlash from both the religious right and feminist left. Always keen to push boundaries, Guccione publishes nude pictures of the first black Miss America, Vanessa Williams, triggering a public outcry while sending sales skyrocketing.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom shares his speedy method for the perfect roast lamb with salsa verde and minty potatoes. For dessert, he makes an apricot pie with almond crumble.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: Gregg heads to the German capital for a 3 day trip. He gets to the heart of Berlin's food scene and gets involved in the beer culture. Between meals he explores the city's often turbulent past and indulges his love of the musical Cabaret!

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick Stein's journey of discovery brings him to the sunnier climes of the Mediterranean, where he explores the French-Catalan legacy of Roussilon on the foothills of the Pyrenees next to the Spanish border. This is the land that celebrates the best of mountain cuisine with its grilled sausages and snails together with the finest fish straight out of the region's famous self-contained sea lakes. What unites them is the local love for Aioli, the pungent garlic mayo, cherished in southern France. In his country kitchen, Rick cooks a hearty dish of pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James encourages everyone to experiment in the kitchen and makes amazing comfort food from everyday larder ingredients. He is joined by special guest Cyrus Todiwala.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James returns home to his native Yorkshire, where he is joined by good friend and multi-award-winning chef Brian Turner.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc rustles up simple, delicious dishes in his Oxfordshire kitchen.

programme.title: River Cottage Christmas
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and the River Cottage team serve up an extra special festive menu with a twist this Christmas. Hugh is ready to celebrate Christmas with a renewed sense of the value of meat - but also the wonders of seasonal vegetables. This year all the meat comes from the most extensively-living, free-ranging animals of them all - wild game - and more specifically those that are viewed by many people as pests. The River Cottage chefs sharpen their knives in pursuit of wild boar, muntjack deer and greylag geese. Head gardener Mark Diacono and Hugh seek to make the most of their veg patch, even in the depths of winter, unearthing some Christmas favourites, as well as some heritage varieties from off the beaten path. Forager John Wright teams up with Pam 'the Jam' Corbin to make some luxury stocking fillers. And the Canteen hosts a '12 Tasters of Christmas' night, packed with ideas to create an array of canapes and little bites, perfect for that festive drinks party.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is sharing four more mouthwatering recipes. His succulent lamb stew is so quick to prep, but with seriously deep flavours; and a fast and furious tasty warm lentil salad.

programme.desc: Gino travels on his Vespa, further South along Puglia's heel to Salento, riding along its Adriatic coast to the port of San Foca.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in France at an enormous foundry where they produce a cast iron pot every five seconds. He follows production of casserole dishes, from the arrival of 20 tonnes of crude iron right through to brightly coloured orange pots. Along the way he tests his mettle by taking a sample of molten iron at 1,550 degree Celsius. With only a heatproof visor and gloves as protection he dips a ladle in to a bubbling cauldron and pours the white-hot sample into a tiny mould. He also discovers that the coloured enamel they protect their pots with is made from glass.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Wigan, at an enormous soup factory where they produce 2 million tins a day. He follows the production of vegetable soup, from a pea harvest in Yorkshire right through to the finished soup going into cans and being dispatched. Along the way he watches as a 5 tonne avalanche of peas, around 7 million individual peas, are frozen within 2 hours of being picked. He mixes up 3 tonnes of veg and 500 gallons of tomato sauce and watches as they're combined and packed into 10 000 tins. He's astonished by a 27.4 metre tall pressure cooking tower and surprised to learn that for every 1 degree drop in temperature in winter the factory observe a 3.5 percent increase in soup sales.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Ireland, at an enormous liqueurs factory where they produce 540,000 bottles a day. He follows the production of cream liqueur, from the arrival of maize to make Irish Whiskey, right through to dispatch of the finished liqueur. It's our longest ever production timeline, taking more than 3 years. programme.desc: Five restaurant critics put their culinary reputations on the line as they battle for the title of MasterChef Critics Champion 2023.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Essex, at an enormous cereal bar factory where they produce 400,000 fruit and nut packed treats a day. He follows production, from the arrival of two tonnes of macadamia nuts all the way through to dispatch. Along the way, he gets hands on with all the ingredients, from nuts to cranberries, sultanas to puffed rice. He learns that it takes a carefully balanced blend of honey and glucose to bind the ingredients together. Too much honey and the bar would be too chewy. Too much glucose and it would set rock solid. A mix of both produces the ideal texture.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve new bakers are set to undertake intricate baking challenges over the next 10 weeks in order to impress judges Matt Moran and Maggie Beer and be crowned Australia's best amateur baker for 2016.

programme.title: Gino's Italy: Secrets of the South
programme.desc: Gino travels on his Vespa, further South along Puglia's heel to Salento, riding along its Adriatic coast to the port of San Foca.

programme.title: Jamie's Family Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver dishes his kitchen secrets for making the very best roast potatoes and exciting Christmas vegetables; plus lovely leftover recipes.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Festive Specials
programme.sub-title: The Great New Year Bake Off
programme.desc: 2022's Maxy, 2021's Jurgen and Maggie and 2020's Mark battle it out to win the first Star Baker of 2024, before seeing the New Year in with a performance from Bone-Afide.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Xmas Masterclass
programme.desc: The Great British Bake Off judges, Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry, take viewers through stunning recipes that all the family can make during the festive season.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon's ultimate home cooking is inspired by French cuisine in this episode, the food Gordon first fell in love with as a chef. His youngest daughter Tilly helps Gordon cook a perfect chicken Fricassee with sauteed potatoes.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: To get it all going, Jamie is sharing his Sicilian tuna pasta, and sticky teriyaki aubergine.

programme.title: Jamie's Family Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie prepares some exciting alternatives to traditional Christmas desserts with a little help from his mum, Sally, including a killer chocolate mousse and a festive strudel.

programme.title: River Cottage Christmas
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and the River Cottage team serve up an extra special festive menu with a twist this Christmas. Hugh is ready to celebrate Christmas with a renewed sense of the value of meat - but also the wonders of seasonal vegetables. This year all the meat comes from the most extensively-living, free-ranging animals of them all - wild game - and more specifically those that are viewed by many people as pests. The River Cottage chefs sharpen their knives in pursuit of wild boar, muntjack deer and greylag geese. Head gardener Mark Diacono and Hugh seek to make the most of their veg patch, even in the depths of winter, unearthing some Christmas favourites, as well as some heritage varieties from off the beaten path. Forager John Wright teams up with Pam 'the Jam' Corbin to make some luxury stocking fillers. And the Canteen hosts a '12 Tasters of Christmas' night, packed with ideas to create an array of canapes and little bites, perfect for that festive drinks party.

programme.title: Jamie: Together at Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie creates a sensational alternative to the traditional Christmas dinner with an epic mixed roast and stuffed pork belly, a veggie crown and a panettone dessert.

programme.title: Prue Leith: My Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue cooks a Colombian dish inspired by her love for travelling and invites former Scottish Conservative leader Ruth Davidson to cook a classic childhood dessert.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Biscuit Week and the bakers make Viennese sandwiches, a retro minty favourite and dramatic puppet theatres. Who'll take centre stage, and who's delivered their final act?

programme.title: James Martin's Christmas Comforts
programme.desc: James transforms Christmas leftovers into scrumptious dishes that are simple to prepare, and great to eat. He is joined in the kitchen by athlete Denise Lewis.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: Chef Michael van de Elzen and restaurateur Ganesh Raj have a battle on their hands to persuade one particularly reluctant toddler to eat well for less.

programme.title: Jamie: Together at Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie creates a sensational alternative to the traditional Christmas dinner with an epic mixed roast and stuffed pork belly, a veggie crown and a panettone dessert.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Cake baker Molly, proves 'that's the way to do it' when she makes a 1 and a half feed wide replica Punch, complete with stage, striped awning and a full cast of edible characters, as a special surprise for a puppeteer at a baking festival. Suzanne creates a towering 2 and three quarter feet high, 5 tier white on white intricate hand piped wedding cake with an unusual peplum shaped base tier, that must provide a dramatic focal point for the lavish event. Phil and Christine tackle another challenging bake as they create a picture perfect working projector cake, full of personal memories to celebrate one couple's 25th wedding anniversary and joint 50th birthdays.

programme.title: Prue Leith: My Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue cooks a Colombian dish inspired by her love for travelling and invites former Scottish Conservative leader Ruth Davidson to cook a classic childhood dessert.

programme.title: Battle of the Critics
programme.desc: Five restaurant critics put their culinary reputations on the line as they battle for the title of MasterChef Critics Champion 2023.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve new bakers are set to undertake intricate baking challenges over the next 10 weeks in order to impress judges Matt Moran and Maggie Beer and be crowned Australia's best amateur baker for 2016.

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Cake Day. The first heat of bakers take on cartoon and time-travel-themed creations.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: A family's spending on snacks, takeaway and soft drinks has got completely out of hand. Will chef Michael van de Elzen and Ganesh Raj succeed in transforming this family's health and wealth?

programme.title: James Martin's Christmas Comforts
programme.desc: From the comfort of his own home, James creates recipes that are packed full of goodness to help you recover from festive overindulgence. Special guest is Glynn Purnell.

programme.title: Jamie's Family Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie concludes his guide to Christmas with his tips for stress-free parties, soups and cheese fondue, as well as some delicious pomegranate shots and incredible mulled cider.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: In this episode Gordon shows us how to make deliciously healthy food. Recipes include brilliant Bircher muesli, stunning Gazpacho soup and his youngest daughter Tilly helps Gordon cook an irresistible healthy Teriyaki Salmon.

programme.title: Jamie: Together at Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie creates a sensational alternative to the traditional Christmas dinner with an epic mixed roast and stuffed pork belly, a veggie crown and a panettone dessert.

programme.title: Masterchef: Champion of Champions
programme.desc: The very first time five of the most memorable amateur champions return to the MasterChef kitchen in a cooking contest. Each of them are back to show how far their food and careers have progressed and to compete for the title.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the biggest cider factory in the world, which produces more than 350 million litres each year. The scale of production blows his mind as at each turn he's confronted with huge machinery and incredible processes. He visits Birchley Farm in Herefordshire, at the heart of cider country, before heading to the mill at Ledbury to help sort the apples. At the factory itself, he learns about the fermentation process and the critical role of yeast, before being shown the vast scale of the bottling process, which sees 833 bottles cleaned and filled every minute. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey explores how the factory is creating new orchards of a sweet apple variety called Scrumptious through the ancient technique of grafting, as well as revealing how a by-product from the brewing industry, CO2, is vital in the manufacture of fire extinguishers.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Missing Screenplay
programme.desc: At a New York film-society party, George introduces Jonathan to filmmaker Jim Jarmusch, a fan of Jonathan's first book, who is interested in having him rewrite a screenplay. Later, after leaving his personalised script in a therapist's office during an ill-advised late-night tryst with a very young party girl, Jonathan convinces Ray to pose as a patient in order to retrieve it. Meanwhile, George is flummoxed when he runs into his sexy ex-wife Priscilla and her arrogant new husband, fellow publishing magnate Richard Antrem.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Stolen Skateboard
programme.desc: Jonathan falls for his latest client, the mother of a boy whose skateboard was stolen by a neighbourhood bully. Meanwhile, Ray agrees to help a lesbian couple start a new family, to his wife Leah's chagrin. And George has a nostalgic 'armpit crush' on a young publicist he hopes to woo at the opening of a new Brooklyn restaurant.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Lonely White Dove
programme.desc: A smitten Russian parolee contracts Jonathan to track down a chanteuse known as 'the Lonely White Dove'. After a vodka-fuelled reconnaissance mission at the Brighton Beach restaurant where the singer works, Jonathan persuades Suzanne, along with Ray and Leah, to join him there for dinner the next night, without revealing his true purpose. Meanwhile, George decides to get in touch with his feminine side as a way to drive sales of his sagging magazines.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Beautiful Blackmailer
programme.desc: Leah hires Jonathan to help find her new dog, 'Little Ray', and Ray joins him in the search, getting some help from a local woman. A reflective George heads to the hospital for surgery.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Stolen Sperm
programme.desc: Concerned over the sudden disappearance of the two lesbians who've been buying his sperm, Ray enlists Jonathan to help track the couple down. Breaking into their apartment, Jonathan learns the pair has flown the coop - but not without leaving behind a clue that both shocks and intrigues Ray. Meanwhile, George ignores Jonathan's warnings by publishing a disparaging editorial about Richard Antrem, sending his publishing rival into a fit of rage at a local bar.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Having picked up the gauntlet thrown down by Richard - and having thwarted a blackmail bid aimed at getting Jonathan to take a dive - the Edition team gear up for a triple-header boxing challenge against their GQ rivals. But with the bouts only hours away, the women in their lives offer some compelling reasons why winning isn't everything.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: When we last dropped in on New York writer, romantic, unlicensed private detective Jonathan Ames he was taking a pounding in the boxing ring while still licking his breakup wounds. Now that his second novel has been completed (and rejected), Jonathan is augmenting his income as a creative-writing teacher, while taking on some new, desperate clients, ranging from a kinky policeman to the publishing rival of his sexagenarian pal, George Christopher (Ted Danson). How he solves anything is a mystery. In Season 2, Jason Schwartzman returns as Jonathan, who has also decided to be a teacher in a night class in creative writing, though few of his students have any idea who he is. He also continues to hang out with (and enlist the help of) his pal, a laconic, hen-pecked cartoonist named Ray (Zach Galifianakis). This season's guests include Oliver Platt, Laila Robins, John Hodgman and Kristen Wiig, all of whom appeared in Season 1. Other guests include Jessica Hecht, F. Murray Abraham, Kristen Johnston, Mary Kay Place, Kevin Bacon, Olympia Dukakis, John Rothman, Domenick Lombardozz, Richard Masur, Allyce Beasley, Finnerty Steeves, Joel Marsh Garland (The Bounty Hunter), Zoe Kazan (It's Complicated) and Jonathan Ames himself.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Jonathan is hired by Richard Antrem, George's nemesis and boxing foe, to investigate Richard's suspicions that his wife Priscilla (George's ex) is having an affair, and Jonathan's insecurities resurface when Stella announces she's not over her first love. George learns he has a new female urologist, while Ray plots to win back Leah by penning a comic book.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: George worries about remaining 'intact' after getting some sobering news from his new urologist, Dr Kenwood, and a bad day for Jonathan gets worse when a pair of S&M henchmen kidnap him, demanding a ransom that calls 'GI' George and Ray to action.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: I've Been Living Like A Demented God!
programme.desc: A professor hires Jonathan to track down a valuable item, while George finds an alibi to explain the results of his drug test. Flushed with cash from his Super Ray comic book, Ray heads to the bar and meets a familiar barfly.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Jonathan attempts to repair the relationship between a poet/limousine driver and his wife, and Ray has a meeting with Kevin Bacon.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Grievous Clerical Error!
programme.desc: Leah hires Jonathan to help find her new dog, Little Ray. Ray joins Jonathan in the search for the dog, getting some inside help from a local woman. A reflective George heads to the hospital for surgery.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Jonathan meets a new client, a dermatologist who asks him to deliver a letter to his intended at an Asian health spa. Jonathan decides to go with George and Ray to celebrate Ray's birthday, which ends up hurting Ray's feelings. George tries to cheer Ray up in the usual manner. At his writing class, Jonathan learns that Louis Greene won a contest with a story based on Jonathan. When Nina comforts him after class, things go a bit further than Jonathan expected.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: After spending the night with Jonathan, Nina announces she wants to move in, but Ray has a crisis so Jonathan rushes over to his place before he and Nina can resolve the issue. Ray's stalker has sent him emails and a voodoo doll. Jonathan enlists the help of Officer Drake to find Ray's stalker. They convince Drake to track the stalker's emails to a specific computer. At Edition, Kathryn informs George that since he doesn't have cancer, he must be re-evaluated by their substance abuse experts. George prefers a sanitarium, but can he handle two months in Arizona?

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: After celebrating the release of his new book The Blonde in the Woods, Jonathan learns some distressing familial news. George reunites with his daughter Emily and meets her new boyfriend. Ray spends time with his biological son Spencer. Meanwhile, Jonathan's latest case leaves him in a precarious position.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: On the run, Jonathan and Ray try to hunt down the person who framed Jonathan. Meanwhile, George covers for them despite his growing cannabis-induced paranoia.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: As a guest on The New Dick Cavett Show, Jonathan runs into his nemesis. George is surprised to hear Bernard and Emily's news, and Ray sets off an AMBER Alert.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: While showing Emily a 'youthful' time, Jonathan discovers a classified ad for a detective impersonating himself. On a night out with Bernard, George learns that Richard Antrem has opened a restaurant aimed at hurting George's restaurant. Ray, in hot water with Leah, finds himself drawn to a lonely woman named Belinda.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: I Keep Taking Baths Like Lady Macbeth
programme.desc: Jonathan and George see a 'friendship' counsellor and George begins taking singing lessons from a delightful pothead named Josephine. Jonathan attempts to win back George's affection and Ray becomes irresistible to women.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: Two Large Pearls And A Bar Of Gold
programme.desc: Jonathan's college sweetheart enlists him to guard her wedding jewels and reminds him of their prenuptial pact. Ray moves in with George, and George warns Josephine about growing too attached.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Rose Hiney connects with Jonathan on some new leads. George and Ray attempt to right their unrightable wrongs.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: Nothing I Can't Handle By Running Away
programme.desc: George throws a wedding for Emily and Bernard; Jonathan is hesitant to break it off with his new lady; Ray retreats into Spencer's arms; Jonathan gets taken hostage; Ray gathers an army of his new-found Super Ray fans.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: After his girlfriend Suzanne moves out because of his failure to stop drinking and smoking pot, between-books novelist Jonathan Ames peruses his favourite Raymond Chandler novel, Farewell, My Lovely, and is inspired to place an ad on Craigslist offering his services as an unlicensed private investigator. Within hours he gets his first case, involving a 19-year-old girl's desperate search for her missing sister. Despite the fact that he should be working on his second novel, and despite the fact that he is on deadline for a magazine piece, Jonathan heads out into the urban night to track down the girl, embracing his new-found noir-detective status. At the end of a very long day, Jonathan earns his fee - and finds he's got another case.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: During an unsuccessful attempt to win back Suzanne, Jonathan gets a phone call and a new case: a woman named Jennifer wants him to tail her boyfriend Gary to see if he's been unfaithful. After learning that he and Jennifer share a common pastime - drinking - Jonathan goes in search of her boyfriend, with uncomfortable results. Meanwhile, Ray brings Jonathan along to his colonic for moral support, and George hatches a knockout plan for Jonathan to help him cover up an unsightly sore before a big speech.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Missing Screenplay
programme.desc: At a New York film-society party, George introduces Jonathan to filmmaker Jim Jarmusch, a fan of Jonathan's first book, who is interested in having him rewrite a screenplay. programme.desc: George throws a wedding for Emily and Bernard; Jonathan is hesitant to break it off with his new lady; Ray retreats into Spencer's arms; Jonathan gets taken hostage; Ray gathers an army of his new-found Super Ray fans.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: Julio obtains a gold oyster earring, then promptly loses it at the club. Julio explains his sensitivity to shapes, colours, numbers and the alphabet to his fellow rideshare passengers.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: Julio fixates on finding the oyster. He starts his search with Skyler, an influencer desperate to stay relevant.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: After learning of a program that would free his mind from his body, Julio gets a lead on the lost oyster from a bartender, who takes him to the former site of the first gay hamster club.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: When Julio repeatedly refuses to participate in a credit card commercial, Vanesja finds him a new opportunity in a superhero movie. In preparation, he goes to a jump rope class.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: As Julio searches for a loophole to avoid getting a proof of existence, Vanesja contemplates her purpose after run-ins with a successful but depressed client and former professor.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: Julio half-heartedly strikes a deal to create a project for a corporation. But when Vanesja gets a surprising call about Julio's rejected work, an exciting new opportunity emerges.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben Epstein and his best friend Cam are destined to succeed, if only they can find the right scheme. Their venture to sell Ben's Wilfredo Gomez branded skateboard decks hasn't gone well because their star spokesperson has disappeared. Needing to get some of their money back Cam talks Ben into a leather jacket hustle, but they are busted before they can make a sale. Meanwhile, Ben's love-life goes from bad to worse when he discovers the hard way that his ex, Rachel, has moved on with a successful hotelier.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Using the money they got via yet another high-interest loan from Rene, Ben and Cam get their hands on the spool of Japanese denim. They hit a series of dead ends in their search to find a manufacturer for their brand of retro 1970s jeans, but after crashing a dinner with a designer, they are granted a meeting. Rachel invites Ben out for sushi and it ends badly when he realises it's a 'moving on' kind of date. Rene tries some grass-roots marketing ideas for his new beverage Rasta Monsta.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben and Cam track down a tailor to make the 1970s inspired jeans sample for 'Crisp', but discover the fee is a firm $1500. Ben goes to his father for a loan, but his pride stops him from going through with it. Rachel, who works at an interior design firm for an eccentric boss named Edie, rethinks her future after reconnecting with a friend who's back for a short visit from her Peace Corps stint in Africa. Eager to get a bigger marketing commitment for his fledgling Rasta Monsta drink, Rene makes a buyout offer his marketing partners better not refuse. Cam peddles Ben's Wilfredo skateboard decks to some rich Upper East Side kids to round up enough money to pay the pattern maker.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: It's Rachel's birthday and Edie announces she's throwing her a dinner party. When plans get crossed, Rachel invites both Darren and Ben to the party. Though Ben has convinced himself he's moved on, he can't bring himself to be the odd man out. Ben agrees to take Kappo for his long-promised night of clubbing so when Gingy alerts Cam and Domingo to her concerns that Ben is having a hard time, they realise their friend needs company.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben and Cam run into denim entrepreneur Pete Powell in town for business and Cam pushes Pete to hook them up with his Japanese buyer Haraki, but Ben then refuses to go to the meeting. After Darren is threatened by Ben's birthday gift of Cheez Doodles, Edie urges Rachel to prove to her new beau that she's moved on. Rachel invites Darren to have sex in the cloisters to make him feel special. Rene tracks down his cousin Tito to retrieve the bag of cash he had him stash while he was in jail. But Tito and his wife have split up and Tito says the cash is in her basement.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Kappo floats Ben and Cam another loan to cover the vintage Ts they need to buy to make their order before Haraki goes back to Japan. Darren takes Rachel to see his childhood home and then asks her to move in with him, although she has suspicions about his sexuality. Rene continues to look for Flaco to get his missing money. When they finally meet up, Flaco insists he didn't take the money.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Cam and Ben promise Rene they can get him his money in 24 hours when they deliver their T-shirts. Rene lets them work off the money by handing out Rasta Monsta drinks, but they abandon their post when they get a call to pick up the shirts. Rachel is ignoring Darren's calls so he goes to Edie directly to offer her a shot at designing his new hotel. After a presentation, they get the job, but Rachel is suspicious of the offer and tells Darren she's not ready to move in with him. Rene has to impress the beverage company's owner before he can get his Rasta Monsta buyout approved.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Cam juggles his new indentured servitude to Rene with trying to work out how to refill the T-shirt order for Haraki. When he hears Rene mention the insurance claim, Cam suspects his cousin may have had something to do with the stolen Rasta Monsta truck. Rachel has Darren and Edie meet her at the hotel and announces she's quitting the hotel gig, her job with Edie and Darren. While Rene sets up the Rasta Monsta bingo night at the church, he takes the opportunity to confess his criminal urges.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: A comedy focusing on the urban adventures of Ben an aspiring designer and Cam, Ben's best friend, free-spirit and would-be mogul, they continue to hustle to get their Crisp line of t-shirts and hoodies off the ground following a (potentially) lucrative trip to Japan via New York. Back from her soul-searching globe-trot, Rachel realises she still has feelings for Ben, but discovers he's seeing Julie, Rene puts up a head-turning Rasta Monsta billboard that incenses his girlfriend and Domingo expands his business beyond dog-walking.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben and Cam find themselves stuck between their new sales rep and a hard place when they have the opportunity to crash a brunch hosted by the well-connected Nancy Frankenburg. On the personal front, Cam is determined to move out of his grandmother's place, and Ben makes an indifferent step toward commitment by giving Julie his spare key. Rene leans on an indebted client to pay up, but not with money, Rachel lands a new job at Biscuit magazine, and lights up with Domingo.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Fashion rep Nancy lands Crisp a promising gig, though not for the money or clientele that Ben and Cam had hoped for. Rachel bestows a lavish gift on Ben after learning that he and Julie may be parting ways, and Cam goes into the dealing business with Domingo to help raise money for a river-view apartment. Rene shoots a Rasta Monsta viral video with Wilfredo Gomez, and gets closer to his girlfriend Debbie.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: At an upstate wedding, Ben's platonic friendship with Rachel is tested by nostalgia and a revelation about the new guy she's been seeing. Meanwhile, Kapo stresses about speaking from the heart in his best man speech. Cam charms Lulu's dad to secure the perfect location (and an unlikely model) for Crisp's new 'lookbook' photo shoot, and Rene seeks to collect on a debt to cover the costs of Rasta Monsta's expanding business (and to buy bling for Debbie).

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: After Nancy puts the kibosh on getting Crisp into a large midwestern retailer, Ben and Cam take matters into their own hands to charm its young out-of-town buyers. Rene tries to make amends with Debbie and prove he can be a stable family man and Rachel goes from feeling lost at Biscuit to alive in Bushwick, all in a single day.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben senses a conflict of interest when Nancy sets Crisp up with a manufacturer named Yosi. Determined to bring some spice to Biscuit, Rachel disregards her boss and pursues an edgy lead. The Feds catch up with Kapo, Lulu warns Cam against selling out and Rasta Monsta incites the ire of the East Flatbush Caribbean League.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben and Cam debate the future of Crisp. Rene hunts down the culprit behind the Rasta Monsta tincture spray, and gives Kapo some advice on how to survive medium security. Rachel commemorates a job setback with 'shrooms and a shvitz'.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Questioning his faith in Nancy and Yosi, Ben tries to find a way to stitch CRISP - and his friendship with Cam - back together. Domingo and Cam try to clear Rasta Monsta's name with the East Flatbush Caribbean League. Rachel seeks out Edie's advice and Kapo has the best prison send-off party ever.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: Julio obtains a gold oyster earring, then promptly loses it at the club. Julio explains his sensitivity to shapes, colours, numbers and the alphabet to his fellow rideshare passengers.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: Julio fixates on finding the oyster. He starts his search with Skyler, an influencer desperate to stay relevant.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: After learning of a program that would free his mind from his body, Julio gets a lead on the lost oyster from a bartender, who takes him to the former site of the first gay hamster club.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: When Julio repeatedly refuses to participate in a credit card commercial, Vanesja finds him a new opportunity in a superhero movie. In preparation, he goes to a jump rope class.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: As Julio searches for a loophole to avoid getting a proof of existence, Vanesja contemplates her purpose after run-ins with a successful but depressed client and former professor.

programme.title: Fantasmas
programme.desc: Julio half-heartedly strikes a deal to create a project for a corporation. But when Vanesja gets a surprising call about Julio's rejected work, an exciting new opportunity emerges.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben Epstein and his best friend Cam are destined to succeed, if only they can find the right scheme. Their venture to sell Ben's Wilfredo Gomez branded skateboard decks hasn't gone well because their star spokesperson has disappeared. Needing to get some of their money back Cam talks Ben into a leather jacket hustle, but they are busted before they can make a sale. Meanwhile, Ben's love-life goes from bad to worse when he discovers the hard way that his ex, Rachel, has moved on with a successful hotelier.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Using the money they got via yet another high-interest loan from Rene, Ben and Cam get their hands on the spool of Japanese denim. They hit a series of dead ends in their search to find a manufacturer for their brand of retro 1970s jeans, but after crashing a dinner with a designer, they are granted a meeting. Rachel invites Ben out for sushi and it ends badly when he realises it's a 'moving on' kind of date. Rene tries some grass-roots marketing ideas for his new beverage Rasta Monsta.

programme.title: How to Make it in America
programme.desc: Ben and Cam track down a tailor to make the 1970s inspired jeans sample for 'Crisp', but discover the fee is a firm $1500. Ben goes to his father for a loan, but his pride stops him from going through with it. Rachel, who works at an interior design firm for an eccentric boss named Edie, rethinks her future after reconnecting with a friend who's back for a short visit from her Peace Corps stint in Africa. Eager to get a bigger marketing commitment for his fledgling Rasta Monsta drink, Rene makes a buyout offer his marketing partners better not refuse. Cam peddles Ben's Wilfredo skateboard decks to some rich Upper East Side kids to round up enough money to pay the pattern maker.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: Pampered aristocrat Stede Bonnet abandons his life of privilege to become a pirate. Struggling to earn the respect of his crew, Stede's fortunes change after a fateful run-in with the infamous Captain Blackbeard.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: Stede battles feelings of guilt while hunting for missing hostages. Lucius makes a surprising discovery.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: Stede conducts business in the Republic of Pirates; Oluwande and Jim face their past.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: As the threat of attack looms, Blackbeard and Stede finally meet. Jim fields questions from an overly curious crew.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.sub-title: The Best Revenge Is Dressing Well
programme.desc: Blackbeard and Stede attend a fancy party. Oluwande and Frenchie embrace false identities. Izzy and Lucius go head-to-head.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: Stede and crew stage a spectacle. Blackbeard struggles to carry out his plan.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: A trip to St Augustine reveals more about Jim's past. Stede leads Blackbeard on an adventure.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: The arrival of Blackbeard's old buddy leaves Stede feeling like the odd man out. As Oluwande mourns a loss, Jim seeks answers from Spanish Jackie.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: Captured and facing trial, Stede and Blackbeard face tough choices and an uncertain future.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.sub-title: Wherever You Go, There You Are
programme.desc: While Stede attempts to re-enter his old life, Blackbeard tends to a wounded heart.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: While Stede adjusts to his new normal, a heartbroken Blackbeard fills the void with daily raids, sparking concern amongst Izzy and the crew.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: Aboard Zheng's ship, Stede, Olu and Black Pete have surprising revelations; Blackbeard's crew plots his revenge.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: The men of the Revenge come together under Zheng's leadership; Stede tries to retrieve Blackbeard, who must face harsh truths.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: Blackbeard encounters some dysfunctional old acquaintances onshore; Stede and Blackbeard must decide how to move forward.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.sub-title: The Curse Of The Searfaring Life
programme.desc: As Blackbeard remains plagued by the distrust of his crew, Izzy schools Stede on how to be an effective captain.

programme.title: Our Flag Means Death
programme.desc: While the crew celebrates Calypso's birthday, Blackbeard faces a violent party crasher seeking revenge.

programme.desc: Blackbeard sifts through plans for his future and his relationship with a newly infamous Stede; Jim schemes to reunite Oluwande with a hesitant Zheng.

programme.desc: Stede, Zheng, Spanish Jackie and the rest of the revenge crew team up to take down a nefarious common enemy threatening the world of piracy.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: In his big return to television, Nathan Fielder explores the lengths one will go to reduce the uncertainties of everyday life, allowing ordinary people to prepare for life's biggest moments.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: Nathan allows an Oregon woman to prepare for motherhood by simulating the experience of raising a child from zero to eighteen.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: While Angela continues to raise her 'son', Nathan goes to new lengths to help a man prepare for a difficult conversation.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: Nathan travels to LA to train actors for his show.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: After a visit from his parents, Nathan struggles to stick up for his values.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: The aftermath of a birthday party causes Nathan to re-evaluate his entire project.

programme.desc: Follow weed dealer The Guy as he helps New Yorkers in their pursuit of life's greatest highs.

programme.title: High Maintenance
programme.desc: A young girl tries to balance her vices and home life; a couple's birthday party veers out of control.

programme.title: High Maintenance

programme.title: High Maintenance

programme.title: High Maintenance
programme.desc: A couple visiting their daughter in Bushwick embraces their urban surroundings while dealing with a less-than-ideal rental that comes with an unwelcome roommate. The Guy delivers to Brenna and Molly's all-female, politically-minded meet-up, where things get dramatic fast.

programme.title: The Rehearsal

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: Nathan allows an Oregon woman to prepare for motherhood by simulating the experience of raising a child from zero to eighteen.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: While Angela continues to raise her 'son', Nathan goes to new lengths to help a man prepare for a difficult conversation.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: Nathan travels to LA to train actors for his show.

programme.title: The Rehearsal
programme.desc: After a visit from his parents, Nathan struggles to stick up for his values.

programme.title: The Rehearsal

programme.title: High Maintenance
programme.desc: On the day of the eclipse, two couples head to the park and make new friends in the process. The Guy says some goodbyes and attempts to make a few life changes.

programme.title: High Maintenance
programme.desc: While spending some time in his RV and away from the city, The Guy gets some upsetting news but makes an intriguing new connection. Cori, meanwhile, is trying to adjust to a new reality without her friend.

programme.title: High Maintenance
programme.desc: After his bike is stolen, regular customer Marty consults The Guy, whose suggestion sends Marty down a surprising path. Bored with her mundane life, Darby finds various ways to unnerve and bewilder strangers.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible – Dead Reckoning
programme.desc: Ethan Hunt and his IMF team embark on their most dangerous mission yet: To track down a terrifying new weapon that threatens all of humanity if falls into the wrong hands. Confronted by a mysterious, all-powerful enemy, Ethan is forced to consider that nothing can matter more than his mission - not even the lives of those he cares about most.

programme.title: Unsung Hero
programme.desc: David Smallbone moves his family from Downunder to the States, searching for a brighter future after his successful music company collapses. With nothing more than their seven children, suitcases, and their love of music, David and his pregnant wife Helen set out to rebuild their lives. David and Helen begin to realise the musical prowess in their children, who would go on to become two of the most successful acts in Inspirational Music history.

programme.title: Dear Santa
programme.desc: A young boy who, in writing his yearly note to Santa, mixes up the letters and sends it to Satan instead.

programme.title: Wonka
programme.desc: Based on the extraordinary character at the center of Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, Wonka tells the wondrous story of how the world's greatest inventor, magician and chocolate-maker became the beloved Willy Wonka we know today. A young Willy Wonka, chock-full of ideas and determined to change the world one delectable bite at a time - proving that the best things in life begin with a dream, and if you're lucky enough to meet Willy Wonka, anything is possible.

programme.title: Bookworm
programme.desc: 11-year-old Mildred's world is turned upside down when her estranged father, the washed-up magician Strawn Wise, comes to look after her and agrees to take her camping to find a mythological beast known as the Canterbury Panther.

programme.title: Maybe I Do
programme.desc: Michelle and Allen are in a relationship and decide to invite their parents to finally meet. Turns out, the parents already know one another well, which leads to some differing opinions about marriage.

programme.title: Anyone But You
programme.desc: Bea and Ben's initial attraction quickly turns sour until they must pretend to be a couple.

programme.title: Mean Girls (2024)
programme.desc: In a musical twist of the cult classic, new student Cady Heron is welcomed by the popular girls called "The Plastics". However, when Cady falls for queen bee Regina's ex-boyfriend, she finds herself in Regina's crosshairs. She must learn to stay true to herself while navigating the most cut throat jungle of all: high school.

programme.title: The Origin of Evil
programme.desc: In France, a woman gets sucked into a world of secrets and betrayal as the battle over her estranged father's massive estate reveals he is more than a genial patriarch.

programme.title: Reality
programme.desc: This film is based on the true story of a government leak from former American intelligence specialist Reality Winner. Saturday afternoon, June 2017, twenty-five-year old Reality Winner is confronted at her home by the FBI. A cryptic conversation begins and soon Reality's life starts to unravel.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Jeanne
programme.desc: Jeanne is headed to Lisbon to put her mother's apartment for sale. At the airport, she bumps into Jean, a whimsical former high school friend.

programme.title: Rock Hudson: All The Heaven Allowed
programme.desc: The biography of renowned actor Rock Hudson is examined in this relevant investigation of Hollywood and LGBTQ+ identity, from his public 'ladies' man' character to his private life as a gay man.

programme.title: The Color Purple (2023)
programme.desc: Torn apart from her sister Nettie and her children, Celie faces many hardships in her life, including an abusive husband simply called Mister. With the support of sultry singer Shug Avery and stand-her-ground stepdaughter Sofia, Celie ultimately finds extraordinary strength in the unbreakable bonds of a new kind of sisterhood.

programme.title: No Hard Feelings
programme.desc: Maddie finds an enticing job ad: parents looking for someone to "date" their awkward son.

programme.title: Ordinary Angels
programme.desc: A struggling hairdresser finds a renewed sense of purpose when she meets a widowed father and his two daughters.

programme.title: Aristotle and Dante
programme.desc: Mexican teenagers Aristotle Mendoza and Dante Quintana explore their friendship while struggling with racial and ethnic identity, sexuality and family relationships in 1987, Texas.

programme.title: The Miracle Club
programme.desc: Four women set out on a journey to Lourdes while confronting their pasts in search of a miracle.

programme.title: Golda
programme.desc: Set during the tense 19 days of the Yom Kippur War in 1973. Israeli Prime Minister Golda Meir is faced with the potential of Israel's complete destruction, and must navigate overwhelming odds, a skeptical cabinet, and a complex relationship with US Secretary of State Henry Kissinger. Her tough leadership and compassion would ultimately decide the fate of her nation and leave her with a controversial legacy around the world.

programme.title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry
programme.desc: Harold is an ordinary man who has passed through life, living on the side lines, until he goes to post a letter one day and just keeps walking.

programme.title: 80 for Brady
programme.desc: Inspired by a true story, four lifelong friends set out on an unforgettable journey to see their hero Tom Brady play in Super Bowl LI and witness one of the greatest comebacks in sports history.

programme.title: My Sailor, My Love
programme.desc: After becoming concerned about her father Howard, Grace hires a caretaker in the form of Annie. As a result of his recluse nature, Howard initially rejects any help from Annie, but gradually the pair begin to bond, and Howard reconsiders opening his heart to love and to be loved once more.

programme.title: My Name is Alfred Hitchcock
programme.desc: A fictional Alfred Hitchcock narrates an explanation of some of the lesser known cinematic techniques he used in his movies, richly illustrated with clips from his entire 50 year career.

programme.title: Northern Comfort
programme.desc: A diverse group of people with a chronic fear of flying are stranded in the wintry north.

programme.title: Master Gardener
programme.desc: A meticulous horticulturist is devoted to tending the grounds of a beautiful estate and pandering to his employer, the wealthy dowager.

programme.title: Ford V Holden
programme.desc: Narrated by Shane Jacobson, this feature-length documentary that explores the decades-long competition between two of Australia's most iconic brands - Ford and Holden. From the early days of the automobile industry to the present, Ford v Holden delves into the history, technology, and culture of these rival companies and their impact on the Australian psyche and automotive landscape. Aussie blood runs red or blue, which one are you?

programme.title: Amber: The Girl Behind The Alert
